items.title: RBI cancels the licence of National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI), vide order dated April 10, 2026, has cancelled the licence of “National Urban Cooperative Bank Ltd., Pratapgarh”, under Section 22 read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 (BR Act). Consequently, the bank ceases to carry on banking business, with effect from the close of business on April 10, 2026. The Commissioner and Registrar of Cooperative, Uttar Pradesh has also been requested to issue an order for winding up the bank and appoint a liquidator for the bank. items.title: RBI launches Quarterly Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey: January – March 2026 (Round 73)
items.summary: he Reserve Bank of India has launched the 73rd round of its Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey (OBICUS). The survey is for the reference period January – March 2026 (Q4:2025-26). The Reserve Bank has been conducting the Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey (OBICUS) of the manufacturing sector on a quarterly basis since 2008. The information collected in the survey includes quantitative data on new orders received during the reference quarter, backlog of orders at the beginning of the quarter, pending orders at the end of the quarter, total inventories

This penalty has been imposed in exercise of powers conferred on RBI under the provisions of Section 47A(1)(c) read with Sections 46(4)(i) and 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949. items.title: Southwest monsoon to be 8% below Long Period Average: IMD
items.summary: India anticipates below-normal monsoon rainfall in 2026. Precipitation is projected to be 8% below the Long Period Average. This forecast raises concerns for crop output and inflation. While some regions may see normal to above-normal rain, most of the country is expected to receive less. The India Meteorological Department will issue updated forecasts in May 2026.

items.title: India, Oman discuss ways to boost trade, investments
items.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with Oman's Minister of Commerce, Industry and Investment Promotion. They discussed ways to enhance trade and investment ties. This follows recent discussions with other Gulf nations. The India-Oman Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement aims to boost mutual growth.

items.title: Treasury yields rise as collapse of Iran talks clouds inflation outlook
items.summary: Treasury yields edged higher on Monday as the breakdown of negotiations between Iran and the U.S. clouded the inflation outlook once again.

items.title: IMD flags below-normal monsoon for 2026; rainfall seen at 92% of long-term average
items.summary: India faces a potential challenge in 2026 with forecasts indicating below-average monsoon rainfall. The India Meteorological Department predicts precipitation at 92% of the long-term average. This outlook raises concerns for agriculture, water resources, and rural economies. The southwest monsoon is vital for irrigating half of India's farmland. A deficit season could impact crop yields, affecting food prices and economic growth.

items.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

items.summary: President Donald Trump had signaled that other countries would help the U.S. in its blockade.

items.title: Coal stock adequate for 90 days available: Union Coal Minister Kishan Reddy
items.summary: “Earlier, we used to have coal adequate for only 21 to 25 days. But, today, more than 200 million tonne of coal adequate for 90 days is there with us," he said

items.title: Nifty is better insulated than you think; here's what Vinod Karki is buying right now
items.summary: Geopolitical tensions are impacting global markets. Vinod Karki, Equity Strategist at ICICI Securities, suggests the Nifty index offers a safe haven. The Nifty's composition, favoring energy producers over consumers, provides resilience. This crisis is seen as a short-term disruption. Largecap companies within the Nifty are better positioned than small and midcaps. Investors should focus on largecaps for better risk-reward.

items.summary: The failure of negotiations at the weekend has raised concerns that the global energy crisis will deepen.

items.summary: The company asserts its commitment to governance and financial stability, stating it is not undergoing insolvency proceedings. Despite a recent 30% stock increase, it faces surveillance measures. The limited exposure of ₹370 crore pertains to a contingent obligation, not financial debt.

items.summary: A global energy crisis is only just beginning. Political turmoil will follow

items.title: Cashfree Payments appoints former Visa executive Sameer Gandhi as CFO
items.summary: Cashfree Payments has appointed Sameer Gandhi as its new Chief Financial Officer, bringing over two decades of experience from global financial institutions. Gandhi's expertise will drive the company's financial strategy as it aims for accelerated growth and profitability. His addition comes after Cashfree Payments secured $53 million in a recent funding round.

items.title: Rupee falls most in two weeks as oil spikes on US move to blockade Iran ports
items.summary: The Indian rupee endured its ​steepest fall in two ​weeks on Monday, as oil prices climbed past $100 per ​barrel, while supportive dollar flows for the currency, spurred by banks' unwinding of arbitrage positions, dissipated.

items.title: Credit penetration among women rise 5-fold to Rs 76 lakh crore between 2017 and 2025
items.summary: Women borrowers have seen a significant five-fold increase in credit access, reaching Rs 76 lakh crore by 2025. This represents 26% of all credit. Business loans for women have grown substantially, indicating a shift towards income-generating activities. Digital sourcing is also playing a key role in improving access for new-to-credit women borrowers.

items.title: India's retail inflation rises to 3.4% in March
items.summary: Rise in prices of food and beverages, and higher energy prices fuelled by West Asia conflict pushed retail inflation higher

items.summary: The earnings of OMCs in Q4 FY26 could be weaker on the back of higher retail fuel losses and a sharp rise in LPG under-recoveries, but partially offset by strong gross refining margins (GRMs).

items.title: Aster DM Healthcare invests Rs 96 crore to expand Whitefield facility; capacity to increase to 539 beds
items.summary: Aster DM Healthcare has invested Rs 96 crore to expand its women and children facility at Aster Whitefield Hospital in Bengaluru. The hospital's bed capacity will increase significantly. This expansion enhances specialized maternity and pediatric care. The initiative aims to improve maternal and child health outcomes in the region. Aster DM Healthcare continues to grow its presence in Bengaluru.

items.title: Top Gainers & Losers on April 13: Ola Electric, Eicher Motors, Swiggy, PB Fintech, HPCL, Meesho among top losers
items.summary: On April 13, the Indian stock market fell nearly 1% as crude prices surged after failed US-Iran peace talks. The Nifty 50 settled at 23,842, while the Sensex dropped to 76,788. Despite sharp losses, indices recovered some declines, indicating support at lower levels.

items.title: India’s March retail inflation quickens to 3.4% as US war on Iran disrupts global trade flows
items.summary: In March, India's retail inflation ticked up to 3.4%, influenced by escalating tensions in the Middle East that are affecting global oil supplies. However, this increase remains comfortably within the target range set by the Reserve Bank of India. While the RBI is aware of external threats, it underscores the resilience of India's economy.

items.title: Amid stock market crash, penny stock hits upper circuit for second straight session. Details here
items.summary: A-1 shares hit the 5% upper circuit for the second day on April 13, following news that over 90% of its fleet is debt-free. The company plans to enhance its fleet and logistics operations to boost competitiveness and revenue, closing at ₹16.31 on the BSE.

items.title: AI without safeguards can amplify existing weaknesses in financial sector: RBI deputy governor
items.summary: Reserve Bank Deputy Governor Swaminathan J cautioned about AI in finance, saying that without proper safeguards, AI adoption can worsen existing issues and introduce new harms. While AI offers benefits like improved customer service and credit delivery, it also presents risks such as bias, opacity and data privacy concerns, he said.

items.title: GTA-maker Rockstar Games hacked again but downplays impact
items.summary: The incident marks the second time the games giant has been hacked by young, English-speaking hackers.

items.summary: Foreign institutional investors (FIIs) quietly increased their exposure to Suzlon Energy in the March quarter, even as they pulled out more than Rs 1 lakh crore from Indian equities during the same period.

items.title: Coal India sets board meeting date to declare Q4 results 2026, final dividend for FY26
items.summary: Coal India's board will meet on April 27, 2026, to approve Q4 FY26 financial results and discuss the final dividend. Analysts predict a weak performance due to competition and demand issues, with expected declines in production, revenue, and EBITDA.

items.title: Stock market holiday on Tuesday, April 14: Are NSE & BSE closed for Dr. Ambedkar Jayanti? Check now
items.summary: NSE BSE Holiday Today: Domestic stock exchanges NSE and BSE will be shut for trading on Tuesday due to Dr. Ambedkar Jayanti holiday. There will be no trading on NCDEX, while MCX will resume trading in the evening session.

items.summary: As geopolitical uncertainties rise, experts suggest a balanced investment strategy focused on diversification, quality equities, and strategic gold allocation to protect portfolios.

items.summary: The proposed relocation of cargo carriers by the Adani Group to its new Mumbai airport has drawn sharp criticism from the United States. The US Department of Transportation has sounded the alarm to India’s aviation ministry, indicating that such a decision could trigger severe consequences for American air transport interests.

items.summary: Haitong has initiated a coverage on One 97 Communication (Paytm) with an ‘Outperform’ rating for a target of Rs 1,410, implying an upside of 26%. The Hong Kong-based brokerage is betting on Paytm's leadership position in retail digital payments in India which is only growing.

items.title: SWITCH Mobility completes deliveries of 100 electric buses to Mauritius
items.summary: In a groundbreaking partnership, SWITCH Mobility has supplied 100 electric buses to the government of Mauritius, making it India's largest export of electric buses to date. This generous donation is more than just a logistical feat; it signifies a pivotal step towards environmentally friendly transport and sustainable urban development in Mauritius, reinforcing its commitment to eco-friendly mobility.

items.summary: The National Stock Exchange’s Rs 20,000 crore IPO is nearing launch, with a June DRHP filing expected. The issue will be a pure OFS, allowing only shareholders who held fully paid-up NSE shares since June 2025 to sell. Eligible investors must file EOIs by April 27, 2026.

items.title: The Rs 1 crore sweet spot: Prestige Group's Irfan Razack says housing demand shows no signs of letting up
items.summary: India's residential real estate market is defying the gloom. Prestige Group crossed the ₹30,000 crore mark in housing sales for the full year, well ahead of its own guidance, and its chairman says the demand engine is still running hot.

items.summary: Ola Electric launched the S1 X+ 5.2 kWh at Rs 1,29,999, making longer-range EVs more accessible. This new variant utilizes Ola's in-house 4680 Bharat Cell technology, aiming to bring high-performance features to the mass market and accelerate EV adoption in India.

items.summary: Rolls-Royce has said the project will create 8,000 jobs across Britain.

items.summary: Record FY26 pre-sales lift listed developers, but war-led uncertainty, cost inflation and affordability concerns cloud FY27 guidance and margin outlook.

items.summary: Pakistan’s stock markets crashed on Monday, with its benchmark index KSE 100 plunging nearly 6,000 points after the ceasefire talks between Iran and US, which were brokered by the country’s Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and army chief Asim Munir, failed to culminate into a peace deal.

items.title: Puravankara shares jump 17% in biggest one-day gain in two years after March quarter business update
items.summary: Puravankara's shares surged 17.3% on April 13 following March quarter update, reporting a 190% increase in sales to ₹3,547 crore. The company also saw a 36% rise in collections, and launched new projects, enhancing its sales pipeline for FY27.

items.summary: In February, the company said it would make London its largest research hub outside of the U.S.

items.summary: Crude had stabilised on optimism that a deal could be reached to reopen the Strait of Hormuz

items.title: Can Trump’s Iran blockade plunge India into an 'everything crisis'?
items.summary: India has so far managed the oil shock from the Iran war through policy buffers, diversified crude sourcing and intervention by the Reserve Bank of India, keeping inflation and supply chains stable. However, the proposed US naval blockade of Iranian ports by Donald Trump risks escalating the situation and disrupting energy flows through the Strait of Hormuz, potentially driving oil prices higher and prolonging the shock.

items.title: Global Markets | European shares slip as Middle East talks collapse, naval blockade looms
items.summary: European shares dropped on ​Monday as expectations of ​a swift resolution to the Middle East conflict ​faded following the breakdown of U.S.-Iran negotiations and Washington's decision to impose a blockade around the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Bank stocks vs consumer discretionary: Sunil Subramaniam’s top watchlist for the summer season
items.summary: Market expert Sunil Subramaniam warns the bottom is yet to be established amid geopolitical tensions and Trump-induced volatility. He notes DIIs are holding back deployment ahead of a crucial earnings season, with flows turning cautious. Investors should stay patient, avoid panic selling, and accumulate systematically, while tracking consumer discretionary trends, banking NIM pressures, and potential private capex surprises.

items.title: Japanese shares end lower as US-Iran talks falter, naval blockade looms
items.summary: Japanese shares ended lower on Monday ​as the U.S.-Iran peace talks ​collapsed over the weekend and the American Navy prepared a ​blockade on Iranian ports, casting fresh doubts about the durability of an ongoing ceasefire.

items.title: Citius TransNet Investment Trust InvIT IPO price band set at ₹99-100 per unit; check key dates, issue details
items.summary: Citius TransNet Investment Trust InvIT IPO is priced between ₹99 and ₹100, with subscription from April 17 to April 21. It allocates 75% to QIBs and 25% to NIIs, aiming to raise ₹1,105 crore for acquisitions and corporate purposes.

items.title: Multibagger stock AGI Infra shows resilience against stock market crash
items.summary: AGI Infra's stock rose over 2% to ₹371.10 after securing regulatory approval for its New Chandigarh housing project. Despite a broader market sell-off, the stock has shown exceptional growth, rising 127% in the past year and over 4,900% in five years.

items.title: Holding multiple mutual funds isn't diversification, says Nikunj Saraf; shares 3 pillars of an ideal portfolio
items.summary: The smart approach in the current market is deploying capital in 3-4 tranches over the next quarter, increasing SIP amounts right now when more units are being bought at lower prices, and focusing on sectors where earnings resilience is visible regardless of crude, says Nikunj Saraf.

items.title: Humyn Labs expands data collection for physical AI with $20 mn push
items.summary: The firm focuses on recording first-person human activity, visuals, and movements. This data collection occurs within commercial, agricultural, and residential environments. The datasets capture how humans physically interact with surroundings to train physical AI systems

items.title: ITC, paints, QSR: Abneesh Roy on what you should buy and avoid right now
items.summary: Consumer sector outlook for FY27 faces challenges from cigarette tax hikes and crude oil shocks. Abneesh Roy of Nuvama Institutional Equities highlights opportunities in Nestle, Marico, Tata Consumer, Titan, Avenue Supermart, and VMart. Quick service restaurants are advised to approach with caution due to rising costs and market fragmentation. Investors should focus on companies with pricing power and earnings visibility.

items.title: Is Anand Rathi’s FY26 growth solely market-driven?
items.summary: For Anand Rathi Wealth, sustained execution is already priced in. The key monitorable is whether operating leverage continues to offset market cyclicality.

items.title: FIIs raise stakes in 8 smallcaps in Q4; stocks surge up to 70% in CY26
items.summary: FIIs boost stakes in select small-caps despite weak markets, backing winners. Twelve stocks deliver 14–69% gains in Q4 FY26, signaling targeted confidence and momentum-driven accumulation across sectors.

items.title: UK will not back blockade of Strait of Hormuz, PM Keir Starmer says
items.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has stated that the UK will not be drawn into a war with Iran, nor will it support a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Starmer emphasized the critical importance of reopening the strait, confirming that all of Britain's efforts are focused on this objective and will continue.

items.title: NSE gets govt approval to name new subsidiary 'National Coal Exchange of India'
items.summary: The National Stock Exchange has received MCA approval to name its proposed subsidiary ‘National Coal Exchange of India Limited’, marking a key step toward launching a coal trading platform. The stock exchange has committed an initial capital infusion of up to Rs 100 crore for the new company. It will hold a 60% stake in the venture, with the remaining 40% stake offered to other shareholders.

items.title: Trump threatens 50% tariffs on China as report suggests plans for arms shipment to Iran
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to impose a 50% tariff on China, after a report on Sunday that Beijing was preparing a weapons shipment to Iran.

items.title: The Hormuz blockade is a throwdown the US can't win
items.summary: A commentary argues that US President Donald Trump may be underestimating Iran’s leverage in the Strait of Hormuz, warning that a blockade is more likely to deepen conflict than force concessions. It says energy blockades are acts of war, slow to take effect, and risk collapsing the fragile ceasefire while hurting the global economy.

items.title: Vikram Solar crosses 10 GW in global deployments
items.summary: Vikram Solar has achieved a significant milestone, deploying 10 GW of solar modules globally. This doubles their previous deployment in just two years. The company's reach extends to 39 countries, with a strong manufacturing base in India. It is also expanding into energy storage solutions.

items.title: Rainfall below normal? How accurate are predictions by Skymet, IMD?
items.summary: With Skymet projecting the highest probability of drought-like conditions in six years, here’s a look at the pattern of predictions by Skymet and IMD and how accurate they are.

items.summary: The combined impact of rising fuel costs due to the West Asia conflict and the delayed ability to raise tariffs creates a significant financial squeeze for Indian telecom companies in the near term.

items.title: Crude surge lifts toy prices ahead of summer rush
items.summary: Toy prices are rising ahead of the summer holidays as the West Asia conflict pushes up crude-linked raw material costs, especially plastics. Industry body The All India Toy Manufacturers Association said prices could increase 10%–40%, with plastic costs surging sharply in recent weeks.

items.title: India's top solar state has renewable projects of about 60 GW awaiting transmission links
items.summary: India's top solar state, Rajasthan, has 60 GW of clean energy projects waiting for transmission links. Planners are struggling to keep pace with rapid build-out. This challenge affects India's aim to double non-fossil fuel power generation by 2030. The Central Transmission Utility of India Ltd is unable to provide systems for these projects.

items.title: Asia markets trade lower as oil surges after U.S. moves to blockade Iran ports
items.summary: Oil prices surged past $100 after U.S.-Iran talks collapsed and Washington moved toward a naval blockade, setting a cautious tone for Asia markets at the open.

items.title: Gold-Silver Ratio rises to 63.7: Which precious metal should you pick amid rising US-Iran war tensions?
items.summary: The gold-silver ratio has risen, reflecting gold's outperformance. Both metals face challenges from geopolitical tensions, rising oil prices, and expectations of prolonged higher interest rates affecting investor behavior.

items.title: Iranian crude returns to India after seven years as tankers dock at key ports
items.summary: Felicity, a large crude carrier operated by the National Iranian Tanker Company, anchored off Sikka on Gujarat's coast late Sunday, carrying about 2 million barrels loaded at Kharg Island in mid-March

items.title: Prudential Plc names Amit Dave as CEO, Abhishek Saraf as COO
items.summary: Prudential Plc has appointed Amit Dave as CEO and Abhishek Saraf as COO for its upcoming health insurance joint venture with HCL Group in India. These appointments, pending regulatory approval, signal the venture's progress towards launching its standalone health insurance operations. The venture, a 70:30 partnership, is nearing final clearance after navigating regulatory hurdles.

items.title: Q4 preview: earnings remain fragile as margins crack under cost pressures
items.summary: India Inc is entering a more demanding phase where revenue growth alone is not enough. As input costs rise and demand turns uneven, the focus is no longer on how fast companies can grow, but how well they can protect margins.

items.title: India Pharma 2026 event: Nadda lays out road map for India to take global pharma leadership role
items.summary: India Pharma Conference: India is set to become a global leader in advanced medicines like biologics and biosimilars, Union Minister J P Nadda said, underlining the need for stronger research and investment. The government is implementing initiatives like Biopharma SHAKTI and PLI schemes to boost domestic manufacturing and self-reliance, he further said.

items.title: Indian 10-year bond yields rise after failed US-Iran talks pump up crude oil prices; CPI inflation data in focus
items.summary: Government bond yields have been rising globally as elevated crude oil prices amid expectations of prolonged US-Iran war raised inflation risks, while the ceasefire reached last week remained fragile.

items.title: Vodafone Idea, Ola Electric, Suzlon Energy— These are among most traded stocks on NSE today, April 13, 2026
items.summary: Most traded stocks today: Vodafone Idea, Ola Electric Mobility, Filatex Fashions, Jaiprakash Power Ventures, and Suzlon Energy were among the most traded stocks, or most active stocks in terms of volume, on the NSE on Monday.

items.title: Anthropic's new Claude Mythos model: A new threat in the waiting for Indian IT stocks?
items.summary: Indian IT stocks may face fresh turbulence as Anthropic’s preview model, Mythos, raises disruption risks. Analysts warn its sharp gains in software engineering tasks mark a “step-jump,” not incremental progress, potentially pressuring valuations. Kotak says the leap could have meaningful implications for IT services firms, narrowing adaptation time and intensifying concerns over near-to-medium term demand, pricing power, and margins.

items.title: From campus to career: The push to make Indian graduates job-ready
items.summary: India's higher education system faces an employability gap, with many graduates lacking job-ready skills. Vivekananda Global University (VGU) in Jaipur is transforming this by embedding innovation, research, and entrepreneurship into its academic structure. Through initiatives like Transdisciplinary Projects and startup incubation, VGU aims to produce job-ready innovators and entrepreneurs, demonstrating that impactful higher education can thrive beyond metropolitan centers.

items.title: Iran slams US plans of blockade of its ports, says no Gulf port will be safe if its maritime security is threatened
items.summary: An Iranian Armed Forces spokesperson called U.S. restrictions on vessels in international waters “illegal” and said they amount to piracy, warning that Iran will enforce a “permanent mechanism” to control the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Ather Energy shares rally 8% while Eicher Motors, Hero MotoCorp shares drop up to 4%. Here's why
items.summary: Delhi's draft EV Policy 2026-2030 aims to boost electric two-wheeler registrations from FY29, offering significant subsidies and tax exemptions. This move is expected to accelerate EV adoption and curb pollution. The policy's incentives are already impacting stock markets, with EV manufacturers seeing gains while traditional bike-makers face declines.

items.title: Cigarette sales take a hit after tax hike: Time to hit 'sell' button on ITC, Godfrey Phillips stocks?
items.summary: Since the tax hike announcement made at the beginning of the year, cigarette stocks have declined between 10-17%. Industry leader ITC has emerged as the top loser as the Nifty 50 stock is down 17.55% at the ₹300 levels currently.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty pare early losses but stay in red at noon; Auto, Financials drag
items.summary: Market breadth on BSE reflected the cautious sentiment. Of 4,364 stocks traded, 1,798 advanced while 2,375 declined and 191 remained unchanged

items.title: FPIs outflows hit ₹1.8 lakh crore YTD. Where are foreign investors headed as they dump Indian stocks?
items.summary: FPIs have pulled out ₹1.8 lakh crore from Indian equities in 2026, influenced by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices. Investments are now focusing on markets like Taiwan and Japan, although mutual fund inflows show positive market signs, depending on future geopolitical developments.

items.title: Hormuz blockade could deepen world’s worst energy crisis — and risk a dangerous misstep
items.summary: The U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz threatens to halt more tanker traffic, sending oil surging and risks drawing China into a widening confrontation with Washington.

items.title: Global Markets | Australian shares slide as US-Iran talks fail, risk appetite sours
items.summary: Australian shares declined as collapsed U.S.-Iran peace talks and a U.S. blockade of Iranian ports soured market sentiment. The S&P/ASX 200 index saw its steepest fall since April 2, with gold stocks leading the losses. Conversely, energy stocks rose on higher crude prices, while Telix Pharmaceuticals surged on a new collaboration.

items.title: Draft CAFE-3 Norms: Govt eases penalties, focuses on carbon credit trading for auto sector
items.summary: Automakers exceeding targets can trade surplus credits with those falling short

items.title: India's March palm oil imports fall 19% to three-month low
items.summary: India’s palm oil imports fell nearly 19% month-on-month in March to a three-month low of 689,462 tonnes, as higher global prices prompted refiners to delay purchases, according to the Solvent Extractors' Association of India.

items.title: Cathie Wood vs Michael Burry: The $11 million Palantir bet that’s splitting Wall Street
items.summary: Cathie Wood bought $11 million worth of Palantir Technologies shares, boosting ARK ETFs’ holdings to over ₹283 crore, even as Michael Burry remains bearish. The funds also sold over $10 million worth of Advanced Micro Devices shares.

items.title: From panic to pricing in: Are markets past 'peak fear and sell-off' despite oil price surge?
items.summary: Investors appear to have already priced in much of the geopolitical risks and are growing less reactive to headlines.

items.title: India unveils new security standards for its digital payments system to drive global adoption
items.summary: The Bureau of Indian Standards has introduced fresh norms covering biometrics, QR codes and digital currency to boost confidence in India's rapidly expanding financial tech ecosystem and take it global.

items.title: India’s data centre market to reach $22 billion by 2030
items.summary: India's data centre market is set for massive growth, expected to reach 22 billion dollars by 2030. This expansion is fueled by cloud computing and artificial intelligence. Significant investments are flowing into the sector, with foreign investors leading the way. Government incentives and cost advantages are making India a prime location for data infrastructure development.

items.title: Small-cap stock Enviro Infra surges 15% despite weak stock market sentiment after this business update
items.summary: Despite the turmoil in the stock market driven by geopolitical tensions, Enviro Infra's shares jumped nearly 15%, boosted by significant project announcements.

items.title: NCLAT adjourns hearing on Vedanta plea against selection of Adani's bid for JAL
items.summary: The National Company Law Appellate Tribunal (NCLAT) on Monday adjourned the hearing on Vedanta Ltd’s petitions challenging Adani Enterprises’ ₹14,535-crore bid for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd due to a change in the bench.

items.title: Ola Electric Mobility share price falls around 8% following weakness in Dalal Street; stock up 61% in April
items.summary: Ola Electric Mobility shares fell nearly 8% on April 13, amid profit booking and weak market sentiment. Despite a strong rally earlier in April, the stock remains under pressure, dropping 25% over the past year. Broader market declines also contributed to the downturn.

items.title: US-Iran war: Why is Singapore stock market among the least affected by Middle East crisis?
items.summary: While global markets falter due to geopolitical tensions, Singapore's stock market remains resilient, driven by robust economic growth. The Singapore dollar has also outperformed several Southeast Asian peers lately, offering safe-haven appeal to investors.

items.title: The $150 crude oil warning: US-Iran conflict sends shockwaves through global energy markets
items.summary: Brent crude surged past $100 as the US announced a naval blockade on Iranian ports, escalating the US–Iran conflict and sparking fears of a massive supply shock. Analysts warn prices could jump to $150 if the blockade persists, with up to 12 million barrels a day at risk and global inflation pressures intensifying.

items.title: Dual risks of monsoon deficit and rising input costs threaten India's rural economy in 2026: Report
items.summary: India's rural economy faces a dual threat in 2026 from a potential below-normal monsoon and rising input costs due to the US-Iran conflict. This combination could significantly impact agricultural output, farmer incomes, and rural demand, potentially leading to increased food inflation and straining government finances.

items.title: How Flipkart turned internal mobility into a leadership pipeline
items.summary: Lateral moves across categories and divisions are a core part of how the company’s employees move into leadership positions. Three executives tell us how a culture that rewards experimentation over pedigree and the freedom to reinvent are shaping Flipkart’s next leaders.

items.title: Crude to cost $150? Trump's Hormuz blockade threat risks oil shock
items.summary: Oil prices could skyrocket to $150 a barrel if the US proceeds with a planned blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, a move that could disrupt up to 12 million barrels per day. Brent crude already surpassed $103 following failed talks with Iran, with traders deeming a full blockade unlikely despite escalating tensions and naval preparations.

items.title: Failure of US-Iran talks set to weigh on risk assets Monday
items.summary: The two sides couldn’t reach an agreement during talks in Pakistan, which is likely to disappoint investors who had added exposure to risk assets last week after the countries announced a ceasefire

items.title: Om Power Transmission IPO Day 3: Subscribed over 80%; GMP and key details
items.summary: Om Power Transmission's Rs 150 crore IPO is nearing its close, with an 81% subscription rate. While retail investors show steady interest, grey market premiums suggest a modest listing gain. The company, an EPC firm in power transmission, aims to use IPO funds for expansion and debt reduction, boasting a strong order book.

items.title: Adani Power, Tata Power to Jaiprakash Power: Why are power stocks rising despite the stock market crash?
items.summary: Power stocks are rising due to increased electricity demand ahead of summer, supported by stable fuel availability. Meanwhile, broader markets are declining due to geopolitical tensions. Investors are optimistic about supply stability, with notable gains in Adani Power, JP Power, etc.

items.title: India bonds slump after US-Iran peace talks falter
items.summary: Indian government bonds experienced a significant downturn Monday as failed U.S.-Iran peace talks sent oil prices soaring. This "risk-off" sentiment impacted the rupee and the Nifty 50, reflecting concerns over inflation and growth. Traders noted a reversal after Friday's bond purchases, anticipating a deal that didn't materialize. Investors now await March inflation data for further market direction.

items.title: TCS, Infosys, Wipro to HCL Technologies: IT stocks crash following sell-off in Indian stock market
items.summary: HCL Technologies, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Persistent Systems, Infosys and Wipro were the top losers in the Nifty IT index, falling over 1% each. On the contrary, Coforge and Mphasis were the only gainers in the index.

items.title: Oil prices are a growth problem
items.summary: Plus, diversification is hard

items.title: Zydus Wellness targets $500 million face wash market with tan removal launch
items.summary: The product is clinically tested across Indian skin types and combines chocolate’s antioxidant properties with Vitamin C-rich cherry extract

items.title: Failure of US-Iran truce talks: Rupee opens 57 paise weaker
items.summary: The rupee, which opened at 93.30 per USD against the previous close of 92.73, is currently trading at 93.3850

items.title: Tata Chemicals, Tata Investment Corp shares rally up to 12% amid Tata Sons IPO buzz
items.summary: Shares of Tata Chemicals and Tata Investment Corporation rose sharply on Monday amid growing buzz around a potential Tata Sons IPO. Support from key Tata Trusts members and stakeholders has raised expectations of value unlocking and improved governance.

items.title: RateGain launches AI-driven hotel marketing certification programme
items.summary: RG Varsity covers 3 curriculum areas: digital marketing fundamentals in AI-driven environments, performance and return on ad spend optimisation, and building a digital revenue strategy

items.title: Ola Electric shares fall 7% on profit booking after 40% three-day rally
items.summary: Ola Electric shares dipped 7% as investors booked profits after a 40% rally. The stock's surge was fueled by in-house battery progress, PLI certification, and a historic milestone of over 1 million cumulative registrations. However, concerns remain regarding profitability and weakening revenue trends.

items.title: ACME Solar share price jumps 7% after HSBC assigns ‘Buy’ call, sees up to 30% upside potential
items.summary: ACME Solar Holdings shares rose nearly 7% after HSBC rated it a 'Buy' with a target of ₹350, suggesting a 30% upside. The company plans a significant capacity increase through long-term power agreements and aims to diversify into renewable energy solutions.

items.title: Coforge clears all regulatory approvals for Encora acquisition, merger expected by month-end
items.summary: CEO Sudhir Singh said every element of anticipated synergies remains on course, and described the combined entity as moving into a higher orbit of growth built on AI-led engineering

items.title: Gold, silver rate today, 13 April: Check live price of 24 kt, 22 kt, 18 kt gold and 10 gm, 100 gm, 1 kg silver
items.summary: Gold, silver rates today, 13 April: Check the live price of gold and silver today in Delhi, Mumbai, Bangalore. Kolkata, Chennai and other major Indian cities.

items.title: Bitcoin stays firm above $71K after CPI-led rally triggers profit booking
items.summary: Bitcoin remains steady above $71,000 amid Middle East tensions. Ethereum sees a slight dip, while some altcoins gain and others fall. Analysts note profit booking after a recent Bitcoin surge. Market capitalization has slightly decreased. Traders are closely monitoring oil prices and bond movements for future direction. The overall market sentiment is cautious.

items.title: Private credit has calmed the credit cycle
items.summary: We’d miss it if it went

items.title: Bill Ackman plays to the market
items.summary: Plus, Nest’s private credit mandate, US small caps jump and Rothko in Florence

items.title: Why pressure is mounting at oil giant BP ahead of its annual general meeting
items.summary: Oil giant BP is bracing for a possible shareholder revolt at its upcoming annual general meeting.

items.title: Jyoti CNC shares crash 16% as France probes subsidiary, seizes bank accounts
items.summary: Jyoti CNC Automation's shares plummeted nearly 16% after French authorities launched a judicial investigation into its subsidiary, Huron Graffenstaden SAS, for suspected export of dual-use technology. The probe led to the seizure of bank accounts and properties, and the temporary restriction of the subsidiary's director general.

items.title: Crude oil futures rise as US moves to blockade Iranian ports
items.summary: US Central Command said its forces will begin implementing a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports on April 13 at 10 a.m. ET.

items.title: DMart share price falls after Emkay Global assigns ‘Sell’ rating; sees 16% downside potential
items.summary: DMart share price has gained over 13% in one month and has rallied 17% on a year-to-date (YTD) basis. The stock has risen 5.5% in one year, but has fallen 9% in two years. DMart shares have surged 54% over the past five years.

items.title: Trent vs DMart: Which retailer’s shares should you buy now?
items.summary: Shares of Trent and Avenue Supermarts have outperformed the market, prompting investors to weigh growth versus stability. Trent offers higher expansion potential through Zudio but with volatility, while DMart provides steadier earnings and stronger downside protection, making it more appealing at current valuations.

items.title: The risks of Trump’s Strait of Hormuz blockade plan
items.summary: Naval embargo designed to cut off Iran’s exports threatens to destabilise global energy markets

items.title: RBI criticises banks’ rupee arbitrage trades
items.summary: Deputy Governor T. Rabi Sankar said the arbitrage between local and offshore markets strained dollar liquidity at a time when the rupee was under pressure due to large foreign outflows

items.title: Acme Solar Holdings shares jump 4% after HSBC initiates buy with 28% upside
items.summary: Shares of Acme Solar Holdings rose sharply on Monday after HSBC initiated coverage with a ‘Buy’ rating and 28% upside. The brokerage expects strong project execution, capacity additions, lower financing costs, and improved cash-flow visibility to drive returns, backed by disciplined bidding and a robust renewable energy pipeline.

items.title: IOC, BPCL, HPCL, Reliance shares drag amid windfall tax hike, rising crude prices
items.summary: IOC shares declined 3.7% in early trade to ₹137.56 on the NSE; Bharat Petroleum fell 5% to ₹284.25, while Hindustan Petroleum dropped 5.4% to ₹340.80 from ₹360.60 and Reliance Industries also slipped nearly 3% to ₹1,311

items.title: Oil prices resume their climb, Asian markets decline as US prepares for blockade of Hormuz
items.summary: Oil prices have been rising as shipping through the strait has essentially stalled since late February

items.title: Sensex, Nifty tumble 2% as US Naval blockade on Iran sends crude surging past $105
items.summary: The sell-off came after US President Donald Trump announced a naval blockade on all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports following the breakdown of diplomatic negotiations

items.title: We are stuck in a currency doom loop
items.summary: Without the dollar, bad situations could be made even worse

items.title: Who lost the UK bond market?
items.summary: The volatility of gilts is a big problem for the country and must be tackled

items.title: Bets against US stocks will have ‘wind at their backs’, says Carson Block
items.summary: Muddy Waters founder says bets against US stocks will have ‘wind at their backs’ as technology upends markets

items.title: UK state-backed fund Nest invests £450mn in US private credit
items.summary: Workplace pension scheme targets 30% allocation to private markets by 2030 despite growing strains on asset class

items.title: TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit on insatiable AI demand
items.summary: TSMC-RESULTS/ (PREVIEW, PIX):PREVIEW-TSMC likely to book fourth straight quarter of record profit on insatiable AI demand

items.title: Eric Swalwell suspends campaign for California governor amid sexual misconduct allegations
items.summary: Multiple women have reportedly accused Democratic Rep. Eric Swalwell of sexual misconduct, among them a former staffer who alleges he sexually assaulted her.

items.title: America’s next Fed chair is caught in a vice
items.summary: Inflation was heating up even before the war in Iran

items.title: US military says it will blockade Iranian ports starting Monday after ceasefire talks ended without agreement
items.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday said the U.S. Navy would begin a blockade of ships linked to Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz after ceasefire talks with Iran in Pakistan failed. The U.S. Central Command said the blockade would start Monday but still allow vessels not heading to Iranian ports to pass through the route.

items.title: Track two: Indian IPO-bound firms explore private sales alongside listing plans
items.summary: More than 10 active IPO mandates have transitioned to this dual-track model, especially for deals in the range of ₹500-2,000 crore, in the past month, according to investment bankers and deal advisors familiar with the matter.

items.title: Nuvama’s PE arm raises focus on manufacturing, invests ₹200 cr in Chennai-based Val-Met Engineering
items.summary: Nuvama Private Equity has acquired a minority stake in Val-Met Engineering for ₹200 crore to capitalize on the aerospace and defence manufacturing boom. The investment aims to scale the specialized material supplier into a global player through tech upgrades and international acquisitions.

items.title: Jhunjhunwalas-backed IKS healthcare looks to acquire TruBridge for $600 million
items.summary: Inventurus Knowledge Solutions (IKS), backed by the family of late investor Rakesh Jhunjhunwala, is in advanced talks to acquire Nasdaq-listed TruBridge for about $600 million. If completed, this would be IKS’s largest acquisition and strengthen its position in healthcare technology and revenue cycle management in the US. A formal announcement is expected soon.

items.title: Air India to offer stock options tied to performance
items.summary: Eligible employees, including pilots, engineers and senior management, will have the right to buy shares later, once they have been granted stock options, at a price between the face value of ₹4 per share and the fair market value on the grant date.

items.title: US-Iran peace talk failure to hit market recovery; rupee; oil may see setback
items.summary: While stocks, currency and energy markets are expected to take a beating, the broader economy appears set for protracted headwinds. Economists warn that a protracted war would raise inflation and interest rates, cooling growth.

items.title: Tata Sons' exit door from upper layer NBFC list narrows
items.summary: New norms, currently a draft circular, take away a part of RBI’s discretion to decid who gets to be on the list. The defining criteria for a non-bank to enter the club is if it has assets of ₹1 trillion or more. Tata Sons had total assets of ₹1.75 trillion on a standalone basis as on 31 March 2025.

items.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
items.summary: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

items.title: Trump says U.S. will blockade Strait of Hormuz after Iran peace talks fail
items.summary: Trump said the U.S. blockade will stop Iran from policing the strait and benefiting economically while the rest of the world suffers from its closure.

items.title: Has the Death of the Department Store Reached Japan?
items.summary: Has the demise of the department store finally arrived in Japan, which has held on to them longer than most?

items.title: High Court relief to Sun Pharma, Ahd Co barred from using 'Esiraft'
items.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries has received a significant legal victory. The Bombay High Court has stopped Meghmani Lifesciences from using its product mark 'Esiraft'. This decision comes after Sun Pharma alleged trademark infringement and passing off rights concerning its brand 'Raciraft'. The court found the marks visually and phonetically similar, likely to cause confusion among consumers.

items.title: Paid music streaming hits the right note as free users fade
items.summary: India's music streaming sector is seeing strong growth. Paid subscribers have increased significantly and are expected to rise further. Affordable plans, telecom bundles, and better payment systems are boosting numbers. Streamers are also focusing on regional content and superior audio quality. Live events are seen as a future differentiator.

items.title: West Asia war: Punjab industry hit by labour shortage, rising costs
items.summary: The ongoing turmoil in West Asia is sending ripples through Punjab's industrial landscape. Manufacturers of textiles, bicycles, and automotive components are grappling with surging production costs and shipment delays. In response, businesses are exploring alternative fuel sources while trying to absorb or mitigate rising expenses.

items.title: NCLT clears merger of Nirmal Lifestyle Realty with Oberoi Realty
items.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has approved the merger of Nirmal Lifestyle Realty with Oberoi Realty. This move will simplify Oberoi Realty's group structure and enhance operational efficiencies. Nirmal Lifestyle Realty will be dissolved without winding up. The amalgamation aims to optimize resource utilization and provide a clearer strategic roadmap.

items.title: Aavas Financiers may be headed for leadership change; Kotak's Manu Singh likely to be Next CEO
items.summary: Aavas Financiers is set for a leadership change. Managing Director Sachinder Bhinder will step down following promoter dissatisfaction. Manu Singh, currently heading home loans at Kotak Mahindra Bank, is expected to become the new CEO. This transition follows CVC Capital Partners' acquisition of control and aims to boost the company's growth trajectory.

items.title: RBI move may push govt NBFCs into Upper-Layer NBFC category
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is proposing new rules for classifying large financial companies. State-owned firms like Power Finance Corp and REC may now be considered Upper-Layer NBFCs. This change aims for clearer regulations based on asset size. The RBI's proposal could bring more entities under stricter oversight.

items.title: FM counters Stalin, denies curbs on paddy incentives
items.summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman has dismissed Tamil Nadu Chief Minister MK Stalin's allegations regarding incentives for paddy cultivation. Sitharaman stated that the Union government's communication was a suggestion to states to align bonus policies with national priorities. The aim was to encourage crop diversification. The decision on state bonuses remains with state governments.

items.title: Prolonged war may hit Rs 75,000 crore dividend target
items.summary: The ongoing turmoil in West Asia casts a shadow over India's revenue streams. Soaring commodity prices may squeeze the profitability of state-run enterprises, which could derail the anticipated ₹75,000 crore dividend. With petroleum sectors particularly at risk, the government is alert to fluctuations in the situation.

items.title: NBFCs doing well, but Iran war, margins cloud road ahead
items.summary: Non-banking finance companies anticipate strong growth in the upcoming quarter. However, rising funding costs may limit profit margins. Analysts are closely monitoring asset quality for potential stress in the coming months. The impact of global events on consumer demand and specific sectors is a key concern. NBFCs are preparing for these challenges.

items.title: Net FDI inflows dip, investors opting for Mexico, Vietnam
items.summary: Officials and experts attribute this trend to the country having to compete with Mexico, Poland, and Vietnam, a preferred part of investors' nearshoring and friendshoring policies after the Covid pandemic, and the US cornering a large share of tech-related investments.

items.title: Diesel export tax hike may align prices of state OMCs, private cos
items.summary: A significant increase in diesel export tax is set to align prices for state-run oil companies and private fuel sellers. This move could end a prolonged dispute over domestic fuel supply agreements. State-run firms typically buy diesel from private refiners and some state-run ones.

items.title: Submit your questions to Alphaville: will Trump blow up the financial dominance of the US?
items.summary: Take part in a live Ask an Expert Q&A with Alphaville’s Robin Wigglesworth and Toby Nangle on Thursday, April 16 at 1pm BST

items.title: Easyjet leaves 100 behind in border check queues
items.summary: About 100 people missed their flights because of border control queues at Milan's Linate airport.

items.title: Tice £91,000 tax row is 'minor administrative error', party claims
items.summary: A property company owned by the Reform UK deputy leader failed to pay £91,000 in tax on dividends, according to the Sunday Times.

items.title: Advisory to states on crop bonus policy to encourage greater crop diversification: FinMin
items.summary: In a statement, the finance ministry said the January 9, 2026, letter from the Department of Expenditure Secretary, Ministry of Finance, to chief secretaries of states was an advisory and not a directive, to align their bonus policy to promote pulses, oilseeds, and millets.

items.title: Could US pensioners be swallowed by the next credit wave?
items.summary: Trump wants retirees’ money in private credit, but it’s a bad idea at this stage of the cycle

items.title: An exclusive 23-story Miami hotel vanishes in a 15-second implosion
items.summary: An exclusive 23-story Miami hotel vanishes in a 15-second implosion

items.title: Coal imports fall 8.5% in February amid high stockpiles, firm global prices
items.summary: India's coal imports saw an 8.5 percent drop in February, reaching 16.55 million tonnes. This decline was driven by high domestic coal stockpiles and firm international prices. The trend is expected to continue as domestic miners work to reduce their inventories. Coal stocks at thermal power plants remain robust, ensuring uninterrupted power generation.

items.title: Patent application filings in India rise 30.2% to 1.43 lakh in 2025-26: Goyal
items.summary: India's patent filings reached a historic high of over 1.43 lakh in FY 2025-26, a 30.2% jump. Domestic innovators led this surge, with Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, and Maharashtra at the forefront. This growth reflects the government's commitment to strengthening intellectual property rights. Initiatives like reduced fees and expedited examination support startups, MSMEs, and educational institutions.

items.title: Leonard Green Partners to buy construction consultancy for $3bn
items.summary: Acquisition comes amid slow patch for private equity dealmaking as interest rates stay high

items.title: 8 out of 10 most India's valuable companies add ₹4.13 lakh crore mcap amid US-Iran ceasefire; HDFC, ICICI Bank top list
items.summary: From the top-10 pack, HDFC Bank, Bharti Airtel, State Bank of India, ICICI Bank, Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), Bajaj Finance, Larsen & Toubro and Hindustan Unilever were the winners, while Reliance Industries and Infosys faced erosion from their valuation.

items.title: TCS makes 25,000 fresher offers in FY27, says more hiring hinges on demand: Report
items.summary: TCS has made 25,000 fresher offers in FY27, down from 44,000 last year, with CEO K Krithivasan telling PTI that further campus hiring will depend on how demand conditions unfold across segments and geographies.

items.title: India-UK free trade pact may come into force from second week of May: Official
items.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement is set to begin in the second week of May. This pact will allow 99 percent of Indian exports to enter Britain duty-free. Tariffs on British products like cars and whisky will also be reduced in India. A separate agreement will prevent double social levies for temporary workers. This aims to boost trade significantly.

items.title: TCS payouts to Tata Sons fall as acquisitions, AI push weigh on cash flows
items.summary: Its latest payout of ₹28,292.1 crore in the financial year ended March 2026 was lower than the previous year’s ₹32,184.2 crore, marking the third decline in total shareholder returns  over the past six years, and the fourth such fall since the company went public in 2004.

items.title: British Steel 'needs nationalising by the summer'
items.summary: Labour MP Nic Dakin says it is "the best outcome" for British Steel as ownership talks continue.

items.title: Lufthansa Crew Strike Set to Cancel More Than 500 Flights
items.summary: Air travelers in Germany face significant disruptions on Friday from a walkout at Deutsche Lufthansa AG that’s set to cause hundreds of flight cancellations at major hubs just as people return from their Easter vacation.

items.title: Commodity traders lost ‘billions’ in early days of Iran war
items.summary: Firms that normally profit from volatility were caught out by sudden rise in energy prices, new report finds

items.title: Aston Martin shares and bonds sink to record lows over cash crunch fears
items.summary: One credit investor says it is unclear who will be luxury-car maker’s latest ‘white knight’

items.title: Wall Street banks set to report $40bn trading haul as Iran war rekindles volatility
items.summary: Five largest US lenders expected to unveil highest combined trading revenues since at least 2014

items.title: How Oracle’s huge AI build-out is causing a cash crunch and layoffs
items.summary: Once a cash-generating software stalwart, Oracle has transformed into a debt-heavy infrastructure builder. The strain is showing.

items.title: Govt sets sail on shipbuilding push with regulator revamp, naval architects
items.summary: The government will appoint naval architects to the Directorate General of Shipping to improve shipbuilding capabilities and reduce dependence on foreign vessels.

items.title: Faisal Islam: Why the government is relaxed about Chinese car imports
items.summary: The UK government believes the rise of China's car industry could be good for UK consumers and industry.

items.title: US-Iran talks at a ‘stalemate’ over Strait of Hormuz, negotiators say
items.summary: Two American warships have passed through the contested waterway for first time since start of war

items.title: India, US deepen energy ties; Nuclear, LPG in focus amid Iran-linked supply concerns
items.summary: India is looking to boost energy ties with the United States. Discussions covered nuclear power, coal gasification, and LPG exports. This move aims to diversify India's energy sources. Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri met with US Energy Secretary Chris Wright. The US ambassador confirmed readiness to cooperate on civil nuclear energy and other areas.

items.title: Pilots' union calls for 48-hour strike at Lufthansa starting April 13
items.summary: The union stated that the airline failed to provide a negotiable offer, prompting a walkout that will affect Lufthansa, Lufthansa Cargo, and Lufthansa CityLine through April 14

items.title: Govt hikes export duty on diesel to ₹55.5/litre; ATF to ₹42/litre from today — Check details
items.summary: The Indian government has on 11 April hiked export duty i.e. windfall tax on diesel to ₹55.5/litre, and on ATF to ₹42/litre, with immediate effect.

items.title: Microplastics in India’s agriculture: A growing problem demanding urgent action
items.summary: A new roadmap by NAAS emphasises national monitoring, improved waste management, and biodegradable alternatives to combat the growing crisis due to microplastics.

items.title: How to help kids form healthy relationships with money: They 'end up in much better financial shape' as adults, psychologist says
items.summary: Amid rising costs and a tighter economic climate, more parents are teaching their kids financial literacy lessons that could serve them well later in life.

items.title: Piyush Goyal holds talks with Saudi Arabia trade minister on stronger supply chains
items.summary: India's Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal met with Saudi Arabia's Trade Minister Majid bin Abdullah Al Qassabi. They discussed strengthening supply chains affected by the West Asia crisis. Both ministers expressed optimism for early progress on the India-GCC Free Trade Agreement negotiations. The discussions also focused on ensuring smooth trade flows and recovering from disruptions.

items.title: Our fisheries exports have risen from Rs 60,000 cr to Rs 68,000 cr after US tariffs: MoS Baghel
items.summary: India's fisheries exports have seen a significant rise, reaching Rs 68,000 crore from Rs 60,000 crore. This growth is attributed to exploring new international markets following US tariff changes. The government is targeting Rs 1 lakh crore in fisheries exports within the next five years. Key export hubs include Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, Odisha, Lakshadweep, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
